system.serviceAreaNotes: Carolina Water Service of North Carolina system using TriRiver Waters Sanford Water Filtration Facility finished water context

next_action.title: Review utility response and consider certified point-of-use filtration
next_action.summary: At least one normalized utility observation is above the selected benchmark, so the user should review the utility's current PFAS response and consider a certified point-of-use option while utility remediation proceeds.
next_action.scope_note: Public water utility interpretation; not a household tap test result.

why_this: Public water utility observations are treated as direct official data for this system.
Current normalized assessment counts: 2 above selected benchmark, 2 present below selected benchmark.
Point-of-use is treated as the first escalation class when a user wants extra ingestion-focused margin.
what_this_does_not_tell_you: Benchmark selection here is the registry-linked benchmark attached to each normalized observation, not a universal federal safety finding.
This is a utility-level signal and does not replace a household tap test.
Whole-house escalation is not the default recommendation from this endpoint.

notes: Upfront cost uses normalized vendor-observed pricing tied to the listed option set.

cadence_notes: Amway eSpring Under the Counter Unit with Aux Faucet primary replacement cadence: every 12 months.
Aquasana Claryum 2-Stage primary replacement cadence: every 6 months.
Aquasana Claryum 3-Stage Max Flow primary replacement cadence: every 6 months.
Aquasana Claryum Direct Connect primary replacement cadence: every 6 months.
Aquasana SmartFlow Reverse Osmosis Carbon Block and Advanced Carbon Block replacements: every 6 months.
Aquasana SmartFlow Reverse Osmosis SmartFlow membrane replacement: every 12 months.
Aquasana SmartFlow Reverse Osmosis SmartFlow remineralizer replacement: every 6 months.
notes: Annual maintenance annualizes normalized replacement media pricing by cadence and adds any normalized recurring membrane or service inputs.

certification_checklist.label: Claim-level listing for the specific PFAS you care about
certification_checklist.detail: Verify the listing record names the PFAS reduction claim directly instead of inferring from a product category.

certification_checklist.label: Match the certification standard to the actual claim
certification_checklist.detail: Use NSF ANSI 53 or an equivalent claim-level listing only when the record explicitly carries the reduction claim.

certification_checklist.label: Check replacement cadence and cartridge capacity before buying
certification_checklist.detail: Cost and maintenance burden change materially if cadence is annual vs more frequent.

best_fit_options.label: Aquasana Claryum 2-Stage
best_fit_options.fit_reason: Certified point-of-use option with direct PFOA/PFOS claim coverage in the normalized listing set.
best_fit_options.not_for_everyone: Not for everyone if the upfront cost or annual cartridge cost is too high for the household.

best_fit_options.label: Aquasana Claryum Direct Connect
best_fit_options.fit_reason: Certified point-of-use option with direct PFOA/PFOS claim coverage in the normalized listing set.
best_fit_options.not_for_everyone: Not for everyone if the upfront cost or annual cartridge cost is too high for the household.

best_fit_options.label: Aquasana Claryum 3-Stage Max Flow
best_fit_options.fit_reason: Certified point-of-use option with direct PFOA/PFOS claim coverage in the normalized listing set.
best_fit_options.not_for_everyone: Not for everyone if the upfront cost or annual cartridge cost is too high for the household.

when_to_escalate: A current utility notice or updated direct result moves one or more contaminants above the selected benchmark.
You need extra ingestion-focused margin and are willing to maintain a certified point-of-use unit.
Your household goal expands beyond drinking and cooking water, which requires a separate whole-house justification review.

decision_input.summary: A direct utility notice or above-reference benchmark relation exists, so the engine can open a certified point-of-use path while keeping utility response and direct notice context in view.
